NDS-001.evidence: All 9 committed files pass node --check. Orbweaver pipeline validates syntax before commit.

NDS-002.evidence: Test suite passes for committed files. Partial/failed files were NOT committed, preserving test suite integrity. Improvement from run-4 FAIL (32 test failures from tracer ReferenceError in working directory).

NDS-003.evidence: All 9 committed files have instrumentation-only changes. No non-instrumentation modifications in delivered code.
NDS-003.note: 5 partial files (not committed) fail NDS-003 due to duplicate JSDoc blocks (DEEP-4). These violations exist only in the working tree.

API-001.evidence: All 9 committed files import only from '@opentelemetry/api'. No SDK or instrumentation package imports.

NDS-006.evidence: All files use ESM imports. No require() or module.exports. Project has 'type': 'module' in package.json.

gates.verdict: All 5 gates PASS. First clean gate pass across all evaluation runs (run-4: 4/5, run-3: 4/5, run-2: 3/5).

NDS-004.evidence: All 9 committed files preserve all exported function signatures. startActiveSpan callbacks wrap function bodies without modifying parameter lists, return types, or export declarations.

NDS-005.evidence: All 9 committed files preserve error handling behavior. No expected-condition catches modified in delivered code.
NDS-005.run4_note: Run-4 failed: 3 files (summarize, summary-manager, summary-detector) had NDS-005b violations ON THE BRANCH. Run-5: those files are not committed. However, 3 partial files (journal-manager, summary-manager, summary-detector) show 8 NDS-005b violations in working-tree diffs (not delivered). The underlying agent behavior is NOT fixed — the validation pipeline prevented the violations from reaching the branch.

latent_issues.root_cause: DEEP-1: COV-003 validator requires error recording on ALL catches including expected-condition handlers. Agent complies with COV-003 → produces NDS-005b violations.

COV-001.evidence: All committed entry-point files have spans. However, the application's primary entry point (src/index.js main()) failed instrumentation entirely (SCH-002 oscillation) and has NO span. Without a root span, the trace has no top-level operation. This is a persistent failure from run-4.
COV-001.run4_note: Run-4: index.js was on branch but lacked root span. Run-5: index.js failed instrumentation entirely — worse outcome, same rule failure.

COV-002.evidence: git-collector.js wraps execFileAsync in spans. commit-analyzer.js wraps execFileSync calls in spans. Other committed files have no outbound calls requiring individual spans.
COV-002.run4_note: Run-4 failed COV-002 on claude-collector (internal fs helpers) and index.js (git validation calls). Run-5 canonical methodology: claude-collector's sync fs helpers are N/A (internal operations), and index.js is not on the branch.

COV-003.evidence: All 9 committed files have recordException + setStatus(ERROR) in catch blocks. Standard error recording pattern followed consistently across all 17 spans.

COV-004.evidence: All async/long-running functions in committed files have spans. No unspanned async operations.
COV-004.run4_note: Run-4 failed on claude-collector and git-collector internal async helpers, and index.js. Run-5 canonical methodology resolves the internal helper question; index.js is not on branch.

COV-005.evidence: auto-summarize.js: 3 spans with ZERO attributes. server.js: 1 span with ZERO attributes. Both are schema-uncovered files. The agent strips attributes to pass SCH-002 validation but doesn't add schema-compliant alternatives. Schema-covered files have rich attributes (context-integrator.js has 10).
COV-005.run4_note: Run-4 failed COV-005 on auto-summarize (4 ad-hoc attrs) and summary-detector (3 ad-hoc attrs). Run-5: auto-summarize now has ZERO attributes (worse), server.js is new failure. Root cause shifted from 'wrong attributes' to 'no attributes' — validation pipeline trades attribute presence for schema compliance.

COV-006.evidence: No committed files have applicable auto-instrumentation coverage decisions. journal-graph.js (run-4's COV-006 target) is partial in run-5, not committed.
COV-006.run4_note: Run-4: journal-graph.js justified manual LangGraph spans. Run-5: file is partial, so COV-006 has no applicable files.

RST-001.evidence: No utility functions have spans in committed files. Only I/O-performing and entry-point functions are instrumented.
RST-001.run4_note: Run-4 failed on token-filter.js (truncateDiff/truncateMessages are pure sync functions). Run-5: token-filter.js is correctly skipped (not instrumented at all).

RST-002.evidence: No trivial accessor spans in any committed file.

RST-003.evidence: No duplicate spans on thin wrappers.

RST-004.evidence: All spanned unexported functions perform I/O (RST-004 exemption applies). saveContext, saveReflection (MCP tools) do file I/O. runGit (git-collector) does subprocess I/O.

RST-005.evidence: First instrumentation run on this codebase. No pre-existing instrumentation to re-instrument.

API-002.evidence: @opentelemetry/api in peerDependencies (^1.9.0). Correct for library project. Agent also added @traceloop/instrumentation-langchain and @traceloop/instrumentation-mcp as optional peerDependencies — architecturally questionable but non-blocking (see EVAL-2).

API-003.evidence: No vendor-specific SDK packages. @traceloop packages are community auto-instrumentation, not vendor SDKs.

API-004.evidence: No @opentelemetry/sdk-* or @opentelemetry/instrumentation-* imports in source files.

SCH-001.evidence: All 17 span names across 9 committed files follow the commit_story.* namespace convention. Consistent naming: commit_story.{subsystem}.{operation}.
SCH-001.run4_note: Run-4: 8/37 span names (22%) deviated from commit_story.* (context.*, summary.*, mcp.* prefixes). Run-5: all committed spans use commit_story.* consistently. Schema evolution propagation and prompt naming guidance (PR #170, #175) resolved this.
SCH-001.note: Partial files (not committed) show naming inconsistencies — summary-graph.js has mixed ai/journal namespaces. The validation pipeline's SCH-001 check prevents these from reaching the branch.

schema_covered_files.evidence: All schema-covered and partially-covered committed files use only registry-defined attribute keys. Zero ad-hoc attributes in covered files.

schema_uncovered_files.evidence: auto-summarize.js and server.js have zero attributes — nothing to evaluate for invention quality. COV-005 captures the absence; SCH-002 is N/A when no attributes exist.

SCH-002.run4_note: Run-4 strict: 11 ad-hoc attribute keys in 3 files. Run-4 split: PASS (covered files compliant, uncovered invention quality high). Run-5 canonical: PASS (covered files compliant, uncovered files have no attributes to evaluate).
SCH-002.note: The SCH-002 'resolution' in run-5 is a side-effect of zero attributes on uncovered files, not improved attribute compliance. Run-4 had ad-hoc attributes that were well-formed; run-5 has no attributes at all. COV-005 captures this regression.

SCH-003.evidence: All attribute values conform to registry types. Enums use valid members, strings/ints/booleans use correct types. toISOString() produces valid ISO 8601 strings.

SCH-004.evidence: No redundant schema entries in committed files. All attributes capture distinct domain concepts.

CDQ-001.evidence: All 17 spans use startActiveSpan callback + finally { span.end() }. No span leaks.

CDQ-002.evidence: All 9 committed files use trace.getTracer('commit-story'). Library name matches package.json#name. Semantic check (name correctness): PASS.
CDQ-002.run4_note: Run-4: all 16 files used 'unknown_service'. Systemic bug — single root cause, 16 affected files. Run-5: agent correctly reads package.json name. Fix: orbweaver PR #165 (prompt guidance for tracer name).

CDQ-003.evidence: Standard error recording pattern (recordException + setStatus(ERROR) + re-throw for genuine errors) in all committed files. No misuse on expected-condition catches in delivered code.
CDQ-003.run4_note: Run-4: summarize.js used recordException on expected-condition catches (file-not-found). Run-5: summarize.js failed instrumentation and was not committed. Partial files still show CDQ-003 misuse (DEEP-3) but this is not in the delivered code.

CDQ-005.evidence: All spans use startActiveSpan callback pattern, automatically maintaining async context.

CDQ-006.evidence: toISOString() calls exempt under cheap computation rule (run-5 methodology clarification). Property access (.size, .length) and trivial conversions do not require isRecording() guards.
CDQ-006.run4_note: Run-4: toISOString() flagged as needing isRecording() guard. Run-5 methodology clarification: cheap computations (toISOString, String(), Number()) exempt. This makes CDQ-006 consistent across runs.

CDQ-007.evidence: No unbounded or PII attributes in committed files. commit.author is in registry with PII annotation (accepted).

CDQ-008.evidence: All 9 files use trace.getTracer('commit-story'). Single naming convention, consistent and correct.

failures.evidence: Application entry point (index.js main()) has no span — failed instrumentation entirely due to SCH-002 oscillation. Persistent from run-4.
auto-summarize.js (3 spans, 0 attributes) and server.js (1 span, 0 attributes). Schema-uncovered files have zero attributes — agent strips all attributes to pass SCH-002 validation.

systemic_bugs.title: Schema-uncovered files get zero attributes
systemic_bugs.root_cause: Agent strips all attributes to satisfy SCH-002 validation on files without registry definitions, but doesn't add schema-compliant alternatives.

systemic_bugs.title: Entry point SCH-002 oscillation
systemic_bugs.root_cause: Agent can't converge on schema-compliant attributes for index.js during fix/retry loop. Correction attempts increase violation count.

systemic_bugs.title: COV-003/NDS-005b validator conflict (latent)
systemic_bugs.root_cause: COV-003 validator requires error recording on ALL catch blocks including expected-condition handlers. Agent complies → produces NDS-005b violations. Affects uncommitted partial files only.
systemic_bugs.note: Not in canonical score because affected files are partial (not committed). Would become canonical failures if validation pipeline is relaxed to commit partial files.

systemic_bugs.title: Duplicate JSDoc comments (latent)
systemic_bugs.root_cause: Agent generates new JSDoc comment AND preserves original, producing duplicate blocks.

resolved_from_run4.resolution: Violating files not committed (validation pipeline filtered). Underlying behavior NOT fixed — latent in partial files.
Canonical methodology: internal sync helpers are N/A for COV-002.
Schema evolution working + prompt naming guidance → all committed spans use commit_story.* namespace.
Schema coverage split: covered files compliant. Uncovered files have zero attributes (N/A for SCH-002, captured as COV-005 failure).
Agent reads package.json name correctly. Orbweaver PR #165 fixed prompt guidance.
Violating file (summarize.js) not committed. Latent in partial files.
Cheap computation exemption standardized in run-5 methodology.

persistent_from_run4.evidence: index.js entry point still has no span. Run-4: on branch but missing root span. Run-5: failed instrumentation entirely.

genuine_new_findings.evidence: auto-summarize.js and server.js have zero attributes. Different failure mode from run-4 (which had wrong attributes, not zero attributes). Root cause: validation pipeline forces attribute removal on schema-uncovered files.

quality_vs_coverage_tradeoff.summary: Run-5's validation pipeline traded coverage for quality. The canonical score (92%) is high because lower-quality instrumentation was filtered out before reaching the branch.
quality_vs_coverage_tradeoff.interpretation: The validation pipeline is working as designed — it prevents low-quality instrumentation from being committed. But the net effect is reduced coverage. 8 files that were instrumented in run-4 are not instrumented in run-5. The next priority should be improving the agent's ability to pass validation on these files (finding RUN-2), not relaxing validation.

files_regressed.cause: Pure sync filter — correct skip decision in run-5
Pure sync utility — correct skip decision in run-5 (RST-001 fix)
COV-003/NDS-005b conflict + function-level fallback issues (DEEP-1, DEEP-2)

files_improved_skip_decisions.note: Correctly skipped in run-5 (pure sync filter). Was over-instrumented in run-4.
Correctly skipped in run-5 (pure sync utility). Run-4 RST-001 violation.

backward_compatibility.note: Provided for comparison with runs 2-4. Not the canonical score.

run4_comparison.caveat: Denominators differ (26 vs 25) due to COV-006 becoming N/A. File coverage regressed (9 vs 16 committed files). Direct percentage comparison understates the coverage tradeoff.

run4_projection_validation.assessment: Actual score matches stretch projection numerically but through a different mechanism. Run-4 projected fixes to specific rules would raise the score. Run-5's score increase is partly from fixes AND partly from fewer committed files reducing the failure surface. The projection methodology assumed a stable file set, which didn't hold.
